instruction: Suppose an arc is intercepted by a central angle of $\theta$ radians in a circle of radius r. The chord associated with that arc is the straight line segment joining the endpoints of the arc. The length of the chord, c, is given by $c=r\sqrt{2-2 \cos(\theta)}$ A manufacturer of stop signs packs the signs into a circular drum, so that the signs fit snugly. Determine the radius of the drum given that length of each edge (chord) of the stop signs is 14 inches. (Recall that stop signs are regular octagons, 8-sided polygons with all sides and angles congruent.) [ANS] inches
